data.title: Devon Energy: Operational Strength, Cost Discipline, and Deleveraging Support Buy Rating and $46 Target
data.description:
In a report released yesterday, Josh Silverstein from UBS reiterated a Buy rating on Devon Energy, with a price target of $46.00.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Josh Silverstein has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that point to both operational strength and balance sheet improvement at Devon Energy. He expects fourth-quarter 2025 cash flow and production to land essentially in line with, or at the high end of, company guidance, indicating strong execution and stable volumes, particularly in oil. He also highlights the company’s multiyear cost reduction efforts, which he believes can lower spending needs in 2026 and 2027 relative to current plans, enhancing free cash flow resilience even after trimming his oil price assumptions. In addition, the firm’s progress in paying down debt — with clear visibility toward roughly $1 billion of reduction and a better leverage profile — supports a more attractive balance sheet and underpins his valuation.

Silverstein further notes Devon’s disciplined capital program, with a level, maintenance-oriented 2026 budget aimed at keeping oil production flat while leaving room for upside from efficiency gains that are not yet baked into guidance. The company’s flexible rig contracting structure across key basins allows it to adjust activity if market conditions weaken, moderating risk. He also anticipates continued share repurchases and ongoing cost optimization, particularly in lowering gathering, processing, and transportation expenses on Delaware gas and NGL output, as incremental drivers of shareholder value. Combining these operational, financial, and capital allocation factors, he maintains a Buy rating and keeps his $46 price target based on an undemanding multiple of projected 2027 EBITDA.

In another report released on January 16, Bank of America Securities also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $46.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Ryanair: Solid Traffic Momentum, Earnings Visibility and Balance Sheet Strength Support Upside to €32.30 Target and Justify Buy Rating
data.description:
Ryanair Holdings, the Industrials sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Muneeba Kayani from Bank of America Securities reiterated a Buy rating on the stock and has a €32.30 price target.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Muneeba Kayani has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Ryanair’s solid operational momentum and earnings outlook. The company is expected to lift its full-year 2026 passenger target after traffic growth in the first nine months already exceeded prior guidance, and the analyst has accordingly raised her own volume forecast. She also anticipates management will introduce a profit-after-tax guidance range that broadly aligns with market expectations, reinforcing confidence in the earnings trajectory. In addition, modest fare growth and higher ancillary revenue per passenger are seen supporting revenue, while cost inflation, including fuel, remains manageable within Ryanair’s low-cost structure.

Muneeba Kayani’s rating is based on the view that the current share price does not fully reflect Ryanair’s earnings power and growth prospects. The stock offers an implied upside to her €32.30 price objective, and trades at a forward earnings multiple roughly in line with its long‑term average despite an attractive forecast EPS compound growth through 2029. She emphasizes Ryanair’s strengthening market position, robust balance sheet with net cash, and healthy cash generation as key supports for the investment case. Taken together, these factors justify reiterating a Buy recommendation on the shares.

In another report released on January 15, Deutsche Bank also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a €33.00 price target.

data.title: Balanced Shareholder Realignment and Undervalued Tender Offer Support Buy Rating on Ferretti S.p.A.
data.description:
Niccolo Guido Storer, an analyst from Kepler Capital , maintained the Buy rating on Ferretti S.p.A.. The associated price target remains the same with €4.10.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Niccolo Guido Storer has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Ferretti S.p.A.’s evolving shareholder structure and upside potential versus the offer price. He interprets KKCG Maritime’s plan to raise its stake to just under 30% as a strategic step to unlock a governance deadlock with the current controlling shareholder, Weichai, ahead of the 2026 board renewal. In his view, KKCG’s growing role, together with other European investors such as Piero Ferrari and Danilo Iervolino, would create a more balanced and supportive ownership base for the existing management team. This improved alignment is seen as positive for strategic continuity and value creation.

At the same time, the EUR 3.5 per share tender offer is positioned below his target price of EUR 4.1 per share, indicating meaningful upside from current levels in his valuation framework. He also notes that investors who tender their shares risk missing further gains should a subsequent control transaction or additional corporate events occur after the initial offer. The fact that Weichai has recently been increasing its stake suggests a strong underlying interest in the asset and reinforces the strategic value of Ferretti. Taken together, these elements support his conviction that the stock remains attractively priced and justify maintaining a Buy recommendation.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
